report_name: South Korea Hits E-Commerce Giant Coupang with Record $409M Fine Over Data Breach

threat_actors.name: Former Coupang Employee
threat_actors.description: A former employee of Coupang who stole an authentication signing key to access and exfiltrate the personal information of 37.55 million individuals. The breach was attributed to the company's poor management of authentication keys and weak access controls.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: A former employee used a stolen authentication signing key to gain unauthorized access to customer data, exploiting the company's weak access controls and negligent key management.

identities.name: Coupang
identities.description: A U.S.-incorporated e-commerce giant operating primarily in South Korea. It was fined a record 624.68 billion won (approx. $409 million) for a data breach affecting 37.55 million customers due to inadequate security safeguards. Its subsidiary, Coupang Fulfillment Service, was also implicated in illegal data collection.
